name: Fight Colorectal Cancer
mission: Learn what colorectal cancer is, how it develops, and the key facts, symptoms, and screening guidelines to know.
theoryOfChange: Fight Colorectal Cancer has a published strategic framework called the Colorectal Cancer Care Initiative (CRCCI), detailed in their Colorectal Cancer Care Report. This initiative is a national coalition uniting patient advocates, physicians, public health champions, and healthcare leaders around data-driven goals to transform colorectal cancer screening, diagnosis, and treatment. The framework establishes clear, actionable goals, such as achieving an 80% screening rate for average-risk patients and ensuring 80% of patients with an abnormal non-invasive screening test receive a follow-up colonoscopy within 90 days. It also aims for 80% of patients to initiate treatment within six weeks of diagnosis, and for 80% of patients diagnosed with stage III or IV CRC to receive biomarker and genetic testing. The report utilizes real-world data, scientific literature, and patient experience to set these goals for improving prevention, diagnosis, and care nationwide.
scoreSummary: Fight Colorectal Cancer shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

all_citations.claim: Fight Colorectal Cancer focuses on public policy and research while maintaining high program efficiency.
The organization reported total revenue of $3,060,301 and holds 12.0 months of working capital.

The organization directs 85.2% of its expenses toward its core programs.
Donation options are available for general charitable giving (sadaqah).

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 61/100 due to its high financial efficiency and strong operational health, though its alignment score is moderated by a lack of specific focus on the Muslim donor community.

Focus is primarily on capacity building and advocacy rather than direct service delivery.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause addresses a critical global health urgency, the alignment for Muslim donors is considered low as there are no specific sadaqah-centric programs or targeted outreach to this community [8]. The organization operates as a secular advocacy group, focusing on legislative change and medical research funding [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high effectiveness in resource management, allocating an 85.2% program expense ratio toward patient education and public awareness [1][5]. Its capacity-building model is supported by a robust financial foundation, maintaining 12.0 months of working capital to ensure long-term stability in its research and policy efforts [2][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Fight Colorectal Cancer empowers a global community to advance policy, research, and patient education in the fight against colon and rectal cancers.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 85.2% ensures the vast majority of funds reach core advocacy and research programs.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.07 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity position with 12.0 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer is a national advocacy organization dedicated to supporting patients and champions through public policy initiatives and medical research [1][2]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $3,060,301 to fund its mission of awareness and patient empowerment [2][5].

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">83.4% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core mission of advocacy and research, exceeding the <cite id="1">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
Fight CRC demonstrates lean operations, spending only <cite id="1">$0.07 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The charity <cite id="11">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite> and holds a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> from Candid.

strengths_deep_dive: Fight CRC's financial health is anchored by a <cite id="1">working capital ratio of 12.0 months</cite>, which is considered highly resilient for an advocacy-focused nonprofit. This stability is paired with a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.9%</cite>, indicating consistent growth in donor support. Their commitment to transparency is evidenced by <cite id="5">regularly published audited financial statements</cite> and a <cite id="1">100% independent board of directors</cite>, ensuring that organizational decisions are made without conflicts of interest.
rich_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer (Fight CRC) is a national advocacy organization dedicated to <cite id="2">empowering a community of patients and champions</cite> to advance policy, research, and education. The organization operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="2">$3,060,301 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their operational model prioritizes direct mission impact, maintaining a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 85.2%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor funds support core initiatives like <cite id="5">research advocacy training and patient support</cite>. Beyond direct programming, Fight CRC serves as a <cite id="2">significant grantmaker</cite>, distributing <cite id="2">$186,573 in grants</cite> to support broader cancer research and collaborative efforts. The organization maintains a <cite id="1">resilient financial position</cite> with <cite id="1">12.0 months of operating reserves</cite>, providing significant stability for its long-term advocacy goals. While the organization is primarily domestic in its spending, it addresses a <cite id="5">critical global health need</cite> by funding research that informs colorectal cancer treatment protocols worldwide.
